data.hyperedge_name: <hyperedge>"This policy is designed for estate planning and estate tax liquidity purposes."
<hyperedge>"No war, aviation, or travel exclusions apply to this policy."
<hyperedge>"Annual premium of $32,400 is guaranteed level for the first 10 policy years."
<hyperedge>"The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights including the right to: Designate and change beneficiaries; Assign the policy; Take policy loans; Surrender the policy."
<hyperedge>"August 20, 2024"
<hyperedge>"The death benefit is payable only upon the death of the SECOND insured to die (survivorship basis)."
<hyperedge>"This option is rarely exercised and primarily exists for divorce or trust modification situations."
<hyperedge>"Upon the death of the second insured, we will pay $2,500,000 to the designated beneficiary, minus any outstanding loans."
<hyperedge>"Premium waiver is not available on joint survivorship policies."
<hyperedge>"This policy is owned by an irrevocable trust to exclude proceeds from the insureds' taxable estates."
<hyperedge>"Projected cash value (based on current assumptions): End of Year 10: $285,000 End of Year 20: $712,000."
<hyperedge>"Neither insured has ownership rights unless specified in the trust document."
<hyperedge>"Exclusions apply to certain conditions or events as defined in Article VII."
<hyperedge>"Proof of both deaths must be submitted: • Death certificate of first insured • Death certificate of second insured • Completed claim forms."
<hyperedge>"Premiums are payable annually on August 20th."
<hyperedge>"The surviving insured becomes a single-life whole life policy for administrative purposes only. Coverage amount and premium remain unchanged."
<hyperedge>"Policy may not be assigned without trustee authorization."

<hyperedge>"Available after policy has been in force for 2 years: Maximum: 90% of cash surrender value; Interest rate: Variable, currently 5.5% per annum; Loans reduce death benefit dollar-for-dollar; Available before or after first death."
<hyperedge>"This policy accumulates cash value on a guaranteed schedule."
<hyperedge>"If either insured commits suicide within 2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to return of premiums paid."
<hyperedge>"Payment will be made within 30 days of receiving complete documentation."
<hyperedge>"Proceeds paid to the trust should not be includable in either insured's estate if the trust is properly structured and maintained."

<hyperedge>"When the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid, but: (a) Policy continues in force (b) Premium payment obligation continues (c) Policy owner receives written confirmation (d) Death certificate must be filed with us."
<hyperedge>"The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for a full refund of premium paid."
<hyperedge>"Upon the first death, the policy owner may elect to split this policy into two separate single-life policies, one on each insured."
<hyperedge>"If the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid and the policy continues in force covering the surviving insured."
<hyperedge>", Connecticut"
<hyperedge>"When the first insured dies, no death benefit is paid, but: (a) Policy continues"
<hyperedge>"Consult with estate planning attorney regarding: Three-year lookback rule if policy transferred to trust; Crummey power requirements; Annual exclusion gift planning."
<hyperedge>"Premium may increase but will never exceed the maximum guaranteed premium of $48,600 per year."
<hyperedge>"Estate Planning Life Assurance Ltd. is headquartered in Hartford, Connecticut."
<hyperedge>"The death benefit is paid only after both insureds have died."
<hyperedge>"Premiums continue after the first death at the same rate."
<hyperedge>"This suicide clause resets if the policy is reinstated."
<hyperedge>"If both insureds die in the same accident or incident and it cannot be determined who died first, the death benefit will be paid immediately."

<hyperedge>"Must elect within 2 years of first death; Each policy's face amount determined by actuarial allocation; Both insureds must be alive at time of first death (available only in rare simultaneous death situations with medical uncertainty); Evidence of insurability may be required."
<hyperedge>"Medical uncertainty may be required as part of the underwriting process."
<hyperedge>"Partial withdrawals are not permitted under this policy type."
<hyperedge>"Chairman & Chief Executive Officer"
<hyperedge>"If payment is not received, you have 60 days to pay before the policy cancels."
<hyperedge>"We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds are living."
<hyperedge>"If deaths occur within 30 days of each other, this will be treated as simultaneous for claim purposes."
<hyperedge>"This is a joint life insurance policy insuring two lives."
